title: Smoke evacuation device remote activation system
abstract: A radio frequency (RF) current sensor for remotely activating a smoke evacuation device in an electrosurgical system includes a sensor body with a cable interfacing sidewall and a retaining member. The cable interfacing sidewall and the retaining member define a retention pocket for receiving a cable communicating RF current. The RF current sensor additionally includes a sensor element for detecting RF current in the cable and a sensor cable in electrical communication with the sensor element. The RF current sensor can operate in at least two modes, depending on whether the cable communicates RF current to a monopolar or bipolar electrosurgical instrument, but regardless of the operation mode, the sensor cable can communicate an activation signal and a current signal derived from the detected RF current to the smoke evacuation device, which activates or adjusts the period of time and/or the smoke evacuation flow rate of a vacuum source.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A radio frequency (RF) current sensor for remotely activating a smoke evacuation device in an electrosurgical system, comprising: a sensor body comprising a cable interfacing sidewall and a retaining member, the cable interfacing sidewall and the retaining member defining a retention pocket configured to receive a cable communicating RF current; a sensor element for detecting RF current in the cable; and a sensor cable in electrical communication with the sensor element, the sensor cable communicating one or more of an activation signal or a current signal derived from the detected RF current to the smoke evacuation device, wherein the RF current sensor is configured to operate in at least two modes: a first mode and a second mode. 2. The RF current sensor as in claim 1 , wherein the retaining member flexes away from the cable interfacing sidewall to admit the cable into the retention pocket. 3. The RF current sensor as in claim 1, wherein the RF current sensor operates in the first mode when the sensor element identifies a single RF current in the cable, the single RF current in the cable being communicated between a signal generator and an active electrode of a monopolar electrosurgical instrument or between a return electrode and the signal generator, and wherein one or more of the activation signal or the current signal are derived from the single RF current when the RF current sensor operates in the first mode. 4. The RF current sensor as in claim 1, wherein the RF current sensor operates in the second mode when the sensor element identifies two RF currentsâ a first RF current flowing in an opposite direction as a second RF currentâ in the cable; wherein the second mode causes the RF current sensor to detect the first RF current with respect to the second RF current; and wherein one or more of the activation signal or the current signal are derived from the first RF current when the RF current sensor operates in the second mode. 5. A method for remote activation of a smoke evacuation device, the method comprising: generating a radio frequency (RF) current at a signal generator; communicating the RF current through a source cable to an electrosurgical instrument; detecting an activation of RF current with an RF current sensor communicatively coupled to a smoke evacuation device; in response to detecting the activation of RF current, communicating an activation signal from the RF current sensor to the smoke evacuation device; receiving the activation signal at the smoke evacuation device; and in response to receiving the activation signal, activating a vacuum source for one or more of a defined period of time or to generate a defined smoke evacuation flow rate. 6. The method as in claim 5, further comprising: subsequent to detecting the activation of RF current, detecting the RF current with the RF current sensor; in response to detecting the RF current, periodically or continuously communicating a current signal from the RF current sensor to the smoke evacuation device, the signal current comprising data associated with the RF current; receiving the current signal at the smoke evacuation device; and in response to receiving the current signal, adjusting one or more of the defined period of time or the defined smoke evacuation flow rate of the vacuum source. 7. The method as in claim 6, further comprising: deriving a treatment power based on one or more of the activation signal or the current signal; and calculating an estimated smoke production based on the treatment power, wherein one or more of the defined period of time or the defined smoke evacuation flow rate is based on the estimated smoke production. 8. The method as in claim 6, wherein one or more of the defined period of time or the defined smoke evacuation flow rate is dependent upon a combination of the activation signal and the current signal. 9. The method as in claim 5, further comprising removably associating the RF current sensor with the source cable. 10. The method as in claim 9, wherein the RF current sensor communicates the activation signal through a sensor cable electrically coupled to the RF current sensor and to the smoke evacuation device. 11. The method as in claim 9, wherein the electrosurgical instrument comprises a bipolar electrosurgical instrument, and wherein detecting the activation of RF current from the source cable comprises identifying two RF currents, and isolating a first RF current from the two RF currents, the first RF current being used to generate the activation signal. 12. The method as in claim 5, further comprising removably associating the RF current sensor with a return cable that is electrically coupled to a return electrode and to the signal generator, wherein the RF current sensor detects the activation of RF current from the return cable, and wherein the RF current sensor communicates the activation signal through a sensor cable electrically coupled to the RF current sensor and to the smoke evacuation device. 13. The method as in claim 5, wherein detecting the RF current comprises detecting the RF current above a lower threshold. 14. The method as in claim 13, wherein the lower threshold is at least 100 mA. 15. An electrosurgical system, comprising: a signal generator producing a radio frequency (RF) current; a source cable electrically coupled to the signal generator and to an electrosurgical instrument, the source cable communicating the RF current from the signal generator to the electrosurgical instrument; a smoke evacuation device comprising a vacuum hose, the vacuum hose being positioned proximate the electrosurgical instrument and configured to evacuate smoke generated by the electrosurgical instrument; and an RF current sensor communicatively coupled to the smoke evacuation device, the RF current sensor activating the smoke evacuation device in response to identifying the RF current. 16. The electrosurgical system as in claim 15, wherein the RF current sensor is removably coupled to the source cable, and wherein the RF current sensor activates the smoke evacuation device after identifying the RF current communicated through the source cable. 17. The electrosurgical system as in claim 15, wherein the electrosurgical instrument comprises a monopolar electrosurgical instrument, and wherein the electrosurgical system further comprises a return electrode and a return cable, the return cable communicating the RF current from the return electrode to the signal generator. 18. The electrosurgical system as in claim 17, wherein the RF current sensor is removably coupled to the return cable, and wherein the RF current sensor activates the smoke evacuation device after identifying the RF current communicated through the return cable. 19. The electrosurgical system as in claim 17, wherein the RF current sensor is integrally formed within the return electrode, wherein the RF current sensor identifies the RF current communicated to the return electrode, and wherein the RF current sensor communicates the communicates a signal through a sensor cable electrically coupled to the return electrode and the smoke evacuation device. 20. The electrosurgical system as in claim 17, wherein the RF current sensor is integrally formed within the smoke evacuation device, the smoke evacuation device being electrically coupled to the return electrode by a first return cable and to the signal generator by a second return cable, and wherein the RF current sensor identifies the RF current communicated from the first return cable to the second return cable.
